title: Augmented reality e-commerce
abstract: An augmented reality e-commerce platform may present 3-dimensional (3D) representations of products to a user in an augmented reality environment. In response to a query from a user that is initiated from within an augmented reality environment, image data and product data for a 3D production representation of the product are retrieved from a data store. The image data and the product data are compiled into an integrated file for rending the 3D product representation. The product data may include points of interest that are to be overlaid onto the 3D product representation rendered via the integrated file. The integrated file is used to render the 3D product representation of the product in the augmented reality environment. The augmented reality environment may show one or more real world physical objects along with the 3D product representation, in which the 3D product representation is overlaid with the points of interest.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. One or more non-transitory computer-readable media storing computer- executable instructions that upon execution cause one or more processors to perform acts comprising: retrieving image data and product data for a product from a data store in response to a query from a user that is initiated from within an augmented reality environment for a 3-dimensional (3D) product representation; compiling the image data and the product data into an integrated file for rending the 3D product representation in the augmented reality environment, the product data including one or more points of interest that are to be overlaid onto the 3D product representation rendered via the integrated file; and providing the integrated file for rendering into the 3D product representation of the product in the augmented reality environment that shows one or more real world physical objects along with the 3D product representation, the 3D product representation being overlaid with the one or more points of interest. 2. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein a point of interest is tailored to the user based on at least one of a purchase history, a prior product browsing history, user demographic information, or a social media expression of the user. 3. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the acts further comprise removing a point of interest from the product data to preclude the point of interest from the being overlaid on the 3D product representation in response to determining the point of interest is ignored by a predetermined percentage of users who purchased the product. 4. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 3, wherein the determining that the point of interest is ignored includes cross 

 reference viewing statistics for the point of interest with purchase data for the product. 5. A computer-implemented method, comprising: retrieving image data and product data for a product that are to be retrieved from a data store in response to a query from a user that is initiated from within an augmented reality environment for a 3-dimensional (3D) product representation; compiling the image data and the product data into an integrated file for rending the 3D product representation in the augmented reality environment, the product data including a point of interest to be overlaid onto the 3D product representation, the point of interest selectable by a user to cause a display of additional details regarding a specific feature of the product along with the 3D product representation in the augmented reality environment, the image data includes at least one of object files, rigging, meshes, textures, or shaders; and providing the integrated file for rendering into the 3D product representation of the product in the augmented reality environment that shows one or more real world physical objects along with the 3D product representation, the 3D product representation being overlaid with the point of interest. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein the query is initiated by a user input detected by an input device, the user input including a specific user gesture, a selection of a virtual icon, a voice command, or a facial movement. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 6, wherein the input device includes at least one of a camera, a microphone, a touch surface, or a wearable sensor. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, further comprising removing a point of interest from the product data to preclude the point of interest from the being overlaid on the 3D product representation in response to determining that the 

 point of interest either fails to cause or distracts a plurality of users from purchasing the product, or adding an additional point of interest to be overlaid on the 3D product representation to the product data in response to determining that a similar point of interest for a comparable product is viewed by a predetermined percentage of users who purchased the comparable product. 9. A system, comprising: one or more processors; and memory including a plurality of computer-executable components that are executable by the one or more processors to perform a plurality of actions, the plurality of actions comprising: retrieving image data and product data for a product from a data store in response to a query from a user that is initiated from within an augmented reality environment for a 3 -dimensional (3D) product representation; compiling the image data and the product data into an integrated file for rending the 3D product representation in the augmented reality environment, the product data including one or more points of interest that are to be overlaid onto the 3D product representation rendered via the integrated file; providing the integrated file for rendering into the 3D product representation of the product in the augmented reality environment that shows one or more real world physical objects along with the 3D product representation, the 3D product representation being overlaid with the one or more points of interest; removing a point of interest from the product data to preclude the point of interest from the being overlaid on the 3D product representation in response to determining the point of interest is ignored by a predetermined percentage of users who purchased the product; and 

 adding an additional point of interest to be overlaid on the 3D product representation to the product data in response to determining that a similar point of interest for a comparable product is viewed by a predetermined percentage of users who purchased the comparable product. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein a point of interest is selectable by a user to cause a display of additional details regarding a specific feature of the product along with the 3D product representation in the augmented reality environment.
